fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Michigan Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to michigan, pharmacy technician compact michigan, michigan pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Michigan Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)
board.mailing_address:
Bureau of Professional Licensing
P.O. Box 30670
Lansing, MI 48909

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Michigan.

steps.title: 1. Meet Eligibility Requirements and Obtain National Certification
steps.description: Before applying for a Michigan state license, applicants must be at least 18 years old and hold current national certification from either the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA) ExCPT. This is a mandatory prerequisite for Michigan licensure.

steps.title: 2. Create a MiPLUS Account
steps.description: Visit the MiPLUS online licensing portal at aca-prod.accela.com/lara/Default.aspx. Click 'Register for an Account' and follow the prompts to create a new user account, then verify your email address as instructed.

steps.title: 3. Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created MiPLUS account. From the MiPLUS home page, select 'Professional Licensing' and then locate and complete the appropriate 'Pharmacy Technician Application' (or 'Licensure by Endorsement (Pharmacy Technician)' if applying by endorsement). Ensure all required fields are accurately filled out.

steps.title: 4. Upload Supporting Documents
steps.description: As part of the online application process within MiPLUS, upload all necessary supporting documentation. This primarily includes proof of your current national certification from either PTCB or NHA ExCPT.

steps.title: 5. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $25.00 through the MiPLUS portal using a credit card or electronic check. Your application will not be processed until the fee is received.

steps.title: 6. Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application by logging into your MiPLUS account. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Bureau of Professional Licensing to avoid delays in processing.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Michigan pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: Michigan lists a $137.70 pharmacy technician application and initial licensure fee, with a $61.20 renewal fee for the two-year cycle.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Michigan have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Michigan?
faqs.answer: Michigan’s current licensing report says pharmacy technician applications average about 90 days from receipt to issuance.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Michigan’s current licensing report says pharmacy technician applications average about 90 days from receipt to issuance.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Michigan
hero.dek: Apply online for a Michigan Pharmacy Technician license through the MiPLUS online licensing portal (aca-prod.accela.com/lara/Default.aspx). Create an account, select 'Professional Licensing', and complete the Pharmacy Technician Application.

official_links.registration_steps: Click 'Register for an Account' on the MiPLUS homepage. Follow the prompts to create a user profile, activate your account via email, and then log in to access the licensing applications.
